references.title: Welcome to the new Linear
references.borrowed: Reduced visual noise, tighter density, clearer sidebar and panel hierarchy, and stronger contrast discipline.
references.rejected: Did not copy Linear's issue-tracker framing, branding, or product-specific navigation taxonomy.

references.source: How we redesigned the Linear UI (part II)
references.title: How we redesigned the Linear UI (part II)
references.borrowed: Consistent header alignment, calmer chrome, and tighter view-to-view continuity.
references.rejected: Did not follow Linear's exact tab styling or theme system.

references.title: Nested Layouts
references.borrowed: Persistent section chrome and same-shell child-route continuity instead of shell flicker.
references.rejected: Did not introduce route-group complexity beyond the existing workspace ledger.

references.title: New dashboard redesign is now the default
references.borrowed: Persistent sidebar logic, mobile-aware continuity, and unified section navigation without detached subpages.
references.rejected: Did not use floating mobile navigation bars or team/project switching metaphors.

references.title: Data table
references.borrowed: Dense row scanning, compact toolbar posture, and stable row grammar under responsive changes.
references.rejected: Did not turn the workspace into a generic batch table with toolbar-heavy controls.

references.title: Accessibility guidance for content
references.borrowed: Direct, service-grade wording and accessible explanatory copy that avoids platform jargon.
references.rejected: Did not copy NHS public-service page composition or marketing-free informational layout directly.

references.title: Typography
references.borrowed: Clear heading hierarchy, restrained emphasis, and readable dense information design.
references.rejected: Did not import NHS branding or public-facing typography classes literally.

references.title: Grid Pattern
references.borrowed: Declared keyboard model discipline, shorter tab sequence, and lawful arrow-key behavior inside dense interactive lists.
references.rejected: Did not force full `grid` semantics onto surfaces where `listbox` or simple regions were the better fit.

references.title: Understanding SC 2.4.11: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
references.borrowed: Focus preservation under sticky regions and shell morphs, with reveal-without-jump behavior.
references.rejected: Did not add exaggerated focus-only spacing that would break density discipline.

references.title: Understanding SC 2.5.8: Target Size (Minimum)
references.borrowed: Lawful target sizing for high-risk and high-frequency actions inside compact professional density.
references.rejected: Did not enlarge every control uniformly at the cost of scan density.
